﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: ArgoCD App of Apps — Multi-Application Management
# WHEN TO USE: Managing multiple ArgoCD Applications from one parent
# PREREQUISITES: ArgoCD installed, Application manifests in Git
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: repoURL, path to Application manifests directory
# RELATED FILES: cd/gitops/argocd/application.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# ArgoCD App of Apps pattern
# One application that manages all other applications
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: app-of-apps
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  project: default

  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/my-org/gitops-repo.git
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: argocd/apps   # Directory containing Application manifests

  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: argocd

  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: ArgoCD Application — Single Application
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying one service via ArgoCD GitOps
# PREREQUISITES: ArgoCD installed, Git repo accessible from cluster
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (ArgoCD uses its own repo credentials)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: repoURL, path, destination namespace
# RELATED FILES: cd/gitops/argocd/app-of-apps.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# ArgoCD Application — single app
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: my-app
  namespace: argocd
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io  # Cascade delete on app removal
spec:
  project: default

  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/my-org/my-app.git
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod

  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc  # In-cluster
    namespace: production

  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true       # Remove resources deleted from Git
      selfHeal: true    # Revert manual changes in cluster
      allowEmpty: false
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=true
      - PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground
    retry:
      limit: 3
      backoff:
        duration: 5s
        factor: 2
        maxDuration: 2m

  # Health check customization
  ignoreDifferences:
    - group: apps
      kind: Deployment
      jsonPointers:
        - /spec/replicas  # Ignore HPA-managed replica count

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: ArgoCD ApplicationSet — Auto-generate Applications
# WHEN TO USE: Creating one ArgoCD Application per service/environment
# PREREQUISITES: ArgoCD installed, ApplicationSet controller
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: repoURL, directory path, template namespace
# RELATED FILES: cd/gitops/argocd/application.yaml
# MATURITY: Beta
# ============================================================

# ArgoCD ApplicationSet — generate apps from a list or Git directories
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: ApplicationSet
metadata:
  name: microservices
  namespace: argocd
spec:
  generators:
    # Git generator: create one app per directory in the repo
    - git:
        repoURL: https://github.com/my-org/gitops-repo.git
        revision: HEAD
        directories:
          - path: services/*

    # List generator: create apps from a hardcoded list
    # - list:
    #     elements:
    #       - service: user-service
    #         namespace: production
    #       - service: order-service
    #         namespace: production

  template:
    metadata:
      name: "{{path.basename}}"
    spec:
      project: default
      source:
        repoURL: https://github.com/my-org/gitops-repo.git
        targetRevision: HEAD
        path: "{{path}}"
      destination:
        server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
        namespace: production
      syncPolicy:
        automated:
          prune: true
          selfHeal: true

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: Flux Kustomization — GitOps Reconciliation
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying Kubernetes manifests via FluxCD
# PREREQUISITES: Flux installed, GitRepository source configured
# SECRETS NEEDED: Git credentials (configured in Flux)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: sourceRef, path, interval, namespace
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Flux Kustomization — reconcile Kubernetes manifests from Git
apiVersion: kustomize.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1
kind: Kustomization
metadata:
  name: my-app
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  interval: 5m      # Reconcile every 5 minutes
  path: ./cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
  prune: true       # Remove resources deleted from Git
  sourceRef:
    kind: GitRepository
    name: my-app-repo

  healthChecks:
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      name: app
      namespace: production

  # Post-build variable substitution
  postBuild:
    substituteFrom:
      - kind: ConfigMap
        name: flux-vars
      - kind: Secret
        name: flux-secrets
        optional: true

﻿# Helm Chart Templates

Generic Helm charts for web applications and microservices.

## Charts

| Chart | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `webapp/` | Generic web application (API + frontend) |
| `microservice/` | Microservice with sidecar support |

## Usage

```bash
# Install to dev
helm install my-app cd/helm/webapp -f cd/helm/webapp/values.dev.yaml

# Upgrade in prod
helm upgrade my-app cd/helm/webapp -f cd/helm/webapp/values.prod.yaml

# Dry run
helm install my-app cd/helm/webapp --dry-run --debug
```

﻿apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app
  labels:
    app: app
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxUnavailable: 0
      maxSurge: 1
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: app
    spec:
      # Security: run as non-root
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 2000

      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:latest  # Overridden per environment
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
          env:
            - name: ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT
              valueFrom:
                configMapKeyRef:
                  name: app-config
                  key: environment
            - name: DB_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: app-secrets
                  key: db-password

          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "128Mi"
              cpu: "100m"
            limits:
              memory: "512Mi"
              cpu: "500m"

          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health/ready
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10

          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health/live
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 20

          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            capabilities:
              drop: ["ALL"]

          volumeMounts:
            - name: tmp
              mountPath: /tmp

      volumes:
        - name: tmp
          emptyDir: {}

﻿# Blue-Green Deployment Pattern
# Two identical environments; switch traffic between them
---
# Blue deployment (currently active)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-blue
  labels:
    version: blue
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
      version: blue
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
        version: blue
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:1.0.0

---
# Green deployment (new version being tested)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-green
  labels:
    version: green
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
      version: green
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
        version: green
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:2.0.0

---
# Service — switch between blue/green by changing selector
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: app
spec:
  selector:
    app: my-app
    version: blue  # Change to "green" to switch traffic
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8080

﻿# Canary Deployment Pattern
# Route small percentage of traffic to new version
---
# Stable deployment (90% of replicas)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-stable
spec:
  replicas: 9
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
      track: stable
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
        track: stable
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:1.0.0

---
# Canary deployment (10% of replicas)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-canary
spec:
  replicas: 1  # 1 canary : 9 stable = ~10% canary traffic
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
      track: canary
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
        track: canary
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:2.0.0

---
# Service selects BOTH stable + canary (no track label)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: app
spec:
  selector:
    app: my-app   # Matches both stable and canary pods
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 8080

﻿# Init Containers Pattern
# Run setup tasks before the main container starts
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      initContainers:
        # Wait for database to be ready
        - name: wait-for-db
          image: busybox:1.36
          command: ['sh', '-c', 'until nc -z db-service 5432; do echo waiting; sleep 2; done']

        # Run database migrations
        - name: db-migrate
          image: my-registry/my-app:latest
          command: ['dotnet', 'ef', 'database', 'update']
          env:
            - name: ConnectionStrings__DefaultConnection
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: app-secrets
                  key: db-connection-string

      containers:
        - name: app
          image: my-registry/my-app:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080

﻿# Kubernetes Manifests

Raw K8s manifests using Kustomize for environment promotion.

## Structure

- `_base/`: Base configuration applied to all environments
- `_overlays/dev|staging|prod`: Environment-specific patches
- `_patterns/`: Advanced deployment patterns (blue-green, canary)

## Usage

```bash
# Preview what will be applied
kubectl kustomize cd/kubernetes/_overlays/dev

# Apply to dev
kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/dev

# Apply to prod
kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
```

# WHEN TO USE: Automating Pulumi infrastructure deployments with PR review

    paths:
      - "cd/pulumi/**"                         # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Pulumi project

    paths:

# Pulumi CD Examples

Copy-paste-ready Pulumi infrastructure-as-code examples using **TypeScript** and a reusable GitHub Actions deploy workflow.

## Structure

```
pulumi/
├── README.md             ← this file
├── deploy.yml            ← GitHub Actions: preview on PR, up on merge
├── aws/                  ← ECS Fargate cluster + service
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── Pulumi.yaml
│   └── Pulumi.prod.yaml
├── azure/                ← AKS cluster + container app
│   ├── index.ts
│   ├── Pulumi.yaml
│   └── Pulumi.prod.yaml
└── gcp/                  ← GKE Autopilot cluster + Cloud Run
    ├── index.ts
    ├── Pulumi.yaml
    └── Pulumi.prod.yaml
```

## Quick Start

1. **Install Pulumi CLI:** `curl -fsSL https://get.pulumi.com | sh`
2. **Pick a cloud folder** — `cd cd/pulumi/aws`
3. **Install deps:** `npm install`
4. **Configure stack:** `pulumi stack init dev && pulumi config set aws:region us-east-1`
5. **Preview:** `pulumi preview`
6. **Deploy:** `pulumi up`

## CI/CD Workflow

The `deploy.yml` workflow follows the same plan-on-PR / apply-on-merge pattern as
the Terraform pipeline:

| Event        | Action           | Manual gate? |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------|
| Pull Request | `pulumi preview` | No — informational |
| Push to main | `pulumi up`      | Yes — requires PR approval |

## Backend State

By default these examples use **Pulumi Cloud** for state management.
To use a self-hosted backend, set `PULUMI_BACKEND_URL`:

```bash
# AWS S3
export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=s3://my-pulumi-state

# Azure Blob
export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=azblob://pulumi-state

# GCS
export PULUMI_BACKEND_URL=gs://my-pulumi-state
```

## Related Files

- `ci/github-actions/terraform/plan-apply.yml` — equivalent Terraform pipeline
- `terraform/` — Terraform versions of the same infrastructure

﻿# Continuous Deployment Templates

Templates for deploying applications to various targets.

## Structure

| Folder | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `kubernetes/` | Raw Kubernetes manifests with Kustomize |
| `helm/` | Helm chart templates |
| `targets/` | Cloud-specific deployment pipelines (AKS, EKS, GKE, App Service, ECS, Lambda, OpenShift) |
| `gitops/` | ArgoCD and Flux GitOps patterns |

## Choosing a Deployment Strategy

| Scenario | Recommendation |
|----------|---------------|
| Full K8s control | `kubernetes/_base/` with Kustomize overlays |
| Helm-managed releases | `helm/webapp/` |
| Azure-native team | `targets/azure-aks/` |
| AWS team | `targets/aws-eks/` or `targets/aws-ecs/` |
| Serverless | `targets/aws-lambda/` |
| GitOps | `gitops/argocd/` or `gitops/flux/` |
| Red Hat OpenShift | `targets/openshift/` |

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: AWS CodePipeline — buildspec.yml for CodeBuild
# WHEN TO USE: When using the codepipeline.yml CloudFormation template
# PREREQUISITES: Dockerfile in the project root
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (injected via CodeBuild environment)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: cd/targets/aws-codepipeline/codepipeline.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================
#
# Place this file in your project root if you prefer an external
# buildspec instead of the inline one in codepipeline.yml.
# Then update the CodeBuild Source in the CloudFormation template:
#   Source:
#     Type: CODEPIPELINE
#     BuildSpec: buildspec.yml
#

version: 0.2

env:
  variables:
    DOCKER_BUILDKIT: "1"          # Enable BuildKit for faster builds

phases:
  install:
    runtime-versions:
      docker: 20                   # <-- CHANGE THIS: match your Docker version needs

  pre_build:
    commands:
      - echo "--- Pre-build phase ---"
      - echo "Logging in to Amazon ECR..."
      - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com
      - IMAGE_URI=$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com/$ECR_REPOSITORY
      - IMAGE_TAG=$CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION
      - echo "Image URI = $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG"

      # Run unit tests before building the image
      # Uncomment and adapt for your stack:
      # - dotnet test                          # .NET
      # - npm ci && npm test                   # Node.js
      # - pip install -r requirements.txt && pytest  # Python
      # - ./gradlew test                       # Java/Gradle

  build:
    commands:
      - echo "--- Build phase ---"
      - echo "Building Docker image..."
      - docker build -t $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG -f Dockerfile .   # <-- CHANGE THIS: Dockerfile path
      - docker tag $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG $IMAGE_URI:latest

  post_build:
    commands:
      - echo "--- Post-build phase ---"
      - echo "Pushing Docker image to ECR..."
      - docker push $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
      - docker push $IMAGE_URI:latest
      - echo "Generating imagedefinitions.json for ECS deploy..."
      - printf '[{"name":"%s","imageUri":"%s"}]' "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG" > imagedefinitions.json
      - cat imagedefinitions.json

artifacts:
  files:
    - imagedefinitions.json
    # - appspec.yml                  # Uncomment if using CodeDeploy (blue/green)
    # - taskdef.json                 # Uncomment if using CodeDeploy (blue/green)

reports:
  # Uncomment to publish test results to CodeBuild Reports
  # test-results:
  #   files:
  #     - "**/*.xml"                 # JUnit, NUnit, pytest XML reports
  #   file-format: JUNITXML
  #   base-directory: test-results   # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to test output

cache:
  paths:
    - "/root/.docker/**/*"           # Docker layer cache
    # - "node_modules/**/*"          # Uncomment for Node.js
    # - "/root/.cache/pip/**/*"      # Uncomment for Python
    # - "/root/.nuget/**/*"          # Uncomment for .NET

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: AWS CodePipeline — Build, Test, Deploy (CloudFormation)
# WHEN TO USE: Teams using AWS-native CI/CD instead of GitHub/GitLab
# PREREQUISITES: CodeStar Connection to repo, ECR repository, ECS/EKS target
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (uses IAM roles)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: cd/targets/aws-ecs/, cd/targets/aws-eks/, terraform/aws-ecs/
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================
#
# This CloudFormation template creates a full CodePipeline with:
#   Source (GitHub/CodeCommit) → Build (CodeBuild) → Deploy (ECS or EKS)
#
# Deploy this template:
#   aws cloudformation deploy \
#     --template-file codepipeline.yml \
#     --stack-name my-app-pipeline \
#     --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM \
#     --parameter-overrides \
#         ProjectName=my-app \
#         RepositoryId=my-org/my-repo \
#         BranchName=main \
#         EcsClusterName=my-cluster \
#         EcsServiceName=my-service
#

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: "CI/CD Pipeline — CodePipeline + CodeBuild → ECS Fargate"

# ============================================================
# PARAMETERS
# ============================================================
Parameters:
  ProjectName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Project name — used for naming all resources"
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  RepositoryId:
    Type: String
    Description: "GitHub repository (org/repo format) or CodeCommit repo name"
    Default: my-org/my-repo        # <-- CHANGE THIS

  BranchName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Branch to trigger the pipeline"
    Default: main

  ConnectionArn:
    Type: String
    Description: "ARN of the CodeStar Connection to GitHub (create in Console → Settings → Connections)"
    # <-- CHANGE THIS: create connection first, then paste ARN

  EcrRepositoryName:
    Type: String
    Description: "ECR repository name for built images"
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  EcsClusterName:
    Type: String
    Description: "ECS cluster to deploy to"
    Default: my-cluster            # <-- CHANGE THIS

  EcsServiceName:
    Type: String
    Description: "ECS service to update"
    Default: my-service            # <-- CHANGE THIS

  ContainerName:
    Type: String
    Description: "Container name in the ECS task definition"
    Default: my-app                # <-- CHANGE THIS

  BuildComputeType:
    Type: String
    Description: "CodeBuild compute type"
    Default: BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
    AllowedValues:
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_SMALL
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_MEDIUM
      - BUILD_GENERAL1_LARGE

Resources:
  # ============================================================
  # S3 Bucket — stores pipeline artifacts between stages
  # ============================================================
  ArtifactBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-pipeline-artifacts-${AWS::AccountId}"
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled
      BucketEncryption:
        ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
          - ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
              SSEAlgorithm: AES256
      LifecycleConfiguration:
        Rules:
          - Id: CleanupOldArtifacts
            Status: Enabled
            ExpirationInDays: 30
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

  # ============================================================
  # IAM Role — CodeBuild
  # ============================================================
  CodeBuildRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-codebuild-role"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: codebuild.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CodeBuildPolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              # CloudWatch Logs
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - logs:CreateLogGroup
                  - logs:CreateLogStream
                  - logs:PutLogEvents
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:logs:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:log-group:/aws/codebuild/${ProjectName}-build*"
              # S3 artifacts
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetBucketAcl
                  - s3:GetBucketLocation
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt ArtifactBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${ArtifactBucket.Arn}/*"
              # ECR — push images
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecr:BatchCheckLayerAvailability
                  - ecr:GetDownloadUrlForLayer
                  - ecr:BatchGetImage
                  - ecr:PutImage
                  - ecr:InitiateLayerUpload
                  - ecr:UploadLayerPart
                  - ecr:CompleteLayerUpload
                Resource: !Sub "arn:aws:ecr:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:repository/${EcrRepositoryName}"

  # ============================================================
  # IAM Role — CodePipeline
  # ============================================================
  CodePipelineRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Sub "${ProjectName}-codepipeline-role"
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: codepipeline.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
      Policies:
        - PolicyName: CodePipelinePolicy
          PolicyDocument:
            Version: "2012-10-17"
            Statement:
              # S3 artifacts
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - s3:GetObject
                  - s3:PutObject
                  - s3:GetBucketVersioning
                Resource:
                  - !GetAtt ArtifactBucket.Arn
                  - !Sub "${ArtifactBucket.Arn}/*"
              # CodeBuild
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - codebuild:BatchGetBuilds
                  - codebuild:StartBuild
                Resource: !GetAtt CodeBuildProject.Arn
              # CodeStar Connection (GitHub)
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - codestar-connections:UseConnection
                Resource: !Ref ConnectionArn
              # ECS deploy
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - ecs:DescribeServices
                  - ecs:DescribeTaskDefinition
                  - ecs:DescribeTasks
                  - ecs:ListTasks
                  - ecs:RegisterTaskDefinition
                  - ecs:UpdateService
                Resource: "*"
              - Effect: Allow
                Action:
                  - iam:PassRole
                Resource: "*"
                Condition:
                  StringEqualsIfExists:
                    iam:PassedToService:
                      - ecs-tasks.amazonaws.com

  # ============================================================
  # CodeBuild Project — builds the Docker image
  # ============================================================
  CodeBuildProject:
    Type: AWS::CodeBuild::Project
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${ProjectName}-build"
      Description: !Sub "Build Docker image for ${ProjectName}"
      ServiceRole: !GetAtt CodeBuildRole.Arn
      TimeoutInMinutes: 15
      Artifacts:
        Type: CODEPIPELINE
      Environment:
        Type: LINUX_CONTAINER
        ComputeType: !Ref BuildComputeType
        Image: aws/codebuild/amazonlinux2-x86_64-standard:5.0
        PrivilegedMode: true      # Required for Docker builds
        EnvironmentVariables:
          - Name: AWS_ACCOUNT_ID
            Value: !Ref AWS::AccountId
          - Name: AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
            Value: !Ref AWS::Region
          - Name: ECR_REPOSITORY
            Value: !Ref EcrRepositoryName
          - Name: CONTAINER_NAME
            Value: !Ref ContainerName
      Source:
        Type: CODEPIPELINE
        BuildSpec: |
          version: 0.2
          phases:
            pre_build:
              commands:
                - echo "Logging in to ECR..."
                - aws ecr get-login-password --region $AWS_DEFAULT_REGION | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin $AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com
                - IMAGE_URI=$AWS_ACCOUNT_ID.dkr.ecr.$AWS_DEFAULT_REGION.amazonaws.com/$ECR_REPOSITORY
                - IMAGE_TAG=$CODEBUILD_RESOLVED_SOURCE_VERSION
            build:
              commands:
                - echo "Building Docker image..."
                - docker build -t $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG .
                - docker tag $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG $IMAGE_URI:latest
            post_build:
              commands:
                - echo "Pushing Docker image..."
                - docker push $IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG
                - docker push $IMAGE_URI:latest
                - echo "Writing image definitions for ECS deploy..."
                - printf '[{"name":"%s","imageUri":"%s"}]' "$CONTAINER_NAME" "$IMAGE_URI:$IMAGE_TAG" > imagedefinitions.json
          artifacts:
            files:
              - imagedefinitions.json
      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

  # ============================================================
  # CodePipeline — Source → Build → Deploy
  # ============================================================
  Pipeline:
    Type: AWS::CodePipeline::Pipeline
    Properties:
      Name: !Sub "${ProjectName}-pipeline"
      RoleArn: !GetAtt CodePipelineRole.Arn
      ArtifactStore:
        Type: S3
        Location: !Ref ArtifactBucket
      Stages:
        # --- Source Stage ---
        - Name: Source
          Actions:
            - Name: GitHubSource
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Source
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: CodeStarSourceConnection
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                ConnectionArn: !Ref ConnectionArn
                FullRepositoryId: !Ref RepositoryId
                BranchName: !Ref BranchName
                DetectChanges: true
                OutputArtifactFormat: CODE_ZIP
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceArtifact

        # --- Build Stage ---
        - Name: Build
          Actions:
            - Name: DockerBuild
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Build
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: CodeBuild
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                ProjectName: !Ref CodeBuildProject
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: SourceArtifact
              OutputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildArtifact

        # --- Manual Approval (optional — remove for auto-deploy) ---
        - Name: Approval
          Actions:
            - Name: ManualApproval
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Approval
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: Manual
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                CustomData: !Sub "Approve deployment of ${ProjectName} to ECS?"
                # NotificationArn: arn:aws:sns:...   # <-- CHANGE THIS: SNS topic for approval emails

        # --- Deploy Stage ---
        - Name: Deploy
          Actions:
            - Name: DeployToECS
              ActionTypeId:
                Category: Deploy
                Owner: AWS
                Provider: ECS
                Version: "1"
              Configuration:
                ClusterName: !Ref EcsClusterName
                ServiceName: !Ref EcsServiceName
                FileName: imagedefinitions.json
              InputArtifacts:
                - Name: BuildArtifact

      Tags:
        - Key: Project
          Value: !Ref ProjectName
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: cloudformation

# ============================================================
# OUTPUTS
# ============================================================
Outputs:
  PipelineUrl:
    Description: "CodePipeline console URL"
    Value: !Sub "https://${AWS::Region}.console.aws.amazon.com/codesuite/codepipeline/pipelines/${ProjectName}-pipeline/view"

  CodeBuildProjectName:
    Description: "CodeBuild project name"
    Value: !Ref CodeBuildProject

  ArtifactBucketName:
    Description: "S3 bucket for pipeline artifacts"
    Value: !Ref ArtifactBucket

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to AWS ECS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying containerised apps to ECS Fargate/EC2
# PREREQUISITES: ECR repository, ECS cluster & service, task definition
# SECRETS NEEDED: AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: ECR repository, ECS cluster/service names, Dockerfile path
# RELATED FILES: docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Deploy to AWS ECS

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  ECR_REPOSITORY: my-app
  ECS_SERVICE: my-service
  ECS_CLUSTER: my-cluster
  CONTAINER_NAME: my-app

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        id: login-ecr
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Build and push to ECR
        id: build
        run: |
          IMAGE_URI=${{ steps.login-ecr.outputs.registry }}/${{ env.ECR_REPOSITORY }}:${{ github.sha }}
          docker build -t $IMAGE_URI -f docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api .
          docker push $IMAGE_URI
          echo "image=$IMAGE_URI" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Download task definition
        run: aws ecs describe-task-definition --task-definition my-task --query taskDefinition > task-definition.json

      - name: Update ECS task definition
        id: task-def
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecs-render-task-definition@v1
        with:
          task-definition: task-definition.json
          container-name: ${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}
          image: ${{ steps.build.outputs.image }}

      - name: Deploy to ECS
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecs-deploy-task-definition@v1
        with:
          task-definition: ${{ steps.task-def.outputs.task-definition }}
          service: ${{ env.ECS_SERVICE }}
          cluster: ${{ env.ECS_CLUSTER }}
          wait-for-service-stability: true

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to AWS EKS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to Amazon EKS via OIDC
# PREREQUISITES: EKS cluster, IAM role for OIDC
# SECRETS NEEDED: AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Cluster name, region, and Kustomize overlay path
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Deploy to AWS EKS

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  EKS_CLUSTER: my-eks-cluster

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Configure AWS credentials (OIDC)
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Update kubeconfig
        run: aws eks update-kubeconfig --name ${{ env.EKS_CLUSTER }} --region ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      - name: Login to ECR
        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@v2

      - name: Deploy with Kustomize
        run: |
          kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
          kubectl rollout status deployment/app -n production

name: Deploy AWS Lambda (Serverless Framework)

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Azure AKS
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to Azure Kubernetes Service via OIDC
# PREREQUISITES: AKS cluster, Azure AD credentials
# SECRETS NEEDED: AZURE_CREDENTIALS
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Cluster name, resource group, namespace, image
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml

name: Deploy to Azure AKS

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: [".NET Docker Build & Publish"]

# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Azure App Service
# WHEN TO USE: Teams not yet on Kubernetes, using Azure PaaS

  push:
    branches: [main]

      - name: Azure Login

# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Deploy to Red Hat OpenShift
# WHEN TO USE: Deploying to OpenShift via service account token
# PREREQUISITES: OpenShift cluster, service account with deploy permissions
# SECRETS NEEDED: OPENSHIFT_SERVER, OPENSHIFT_TOKEN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Server URL, project/namespace, image, app name
# RELATED FILES: cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod/kustomization.yaml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Deploy to OpenShift

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

env:
  OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE: production
  APP_NAME: my-app
  IMAGE: image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/production/my-app

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to OpenShift
        uses: redhat-actions/oc-login@v1
        with:
          openshift_server_url: ${{ secrets.OPENSHIFT_SERVER }}
          openshift_token: ${{ secrets.OPENSHIFT_TOKEN }}
          insecure_skip_tls_verify: false

      - name: Set project
        run: oc project ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }}

      - name: Deploy with Kustomize
        run: |
          kubectl apply -k cd/kubernetes/_overlays/prod
          kubectl rollout status deployment/${{ env.APP_NAME }} -n ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }}

      - name: Verify rollout
        run: oc get pods -n ${{ env.OPENSHIFT_NAMESPACE }} -l app=${{ env.APP_NAME }}

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: Azure Pipelines — Terraform Plan / Apply
# WHEN TO USE: Automating Terraform workflows in Azure DevOps
# PREREQUISITES: Azure service connection, Terraform extension installed
# SECRETS NEEDED: Azure service connection (or variable group linked to Key Vault)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: terraform/azure-aks/, terraform/aws-eks/, terraform/gcp-gke/
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

trigger:
  branches:
    include:
      - main
  paths:
    include:
      - terraform/**                # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Terraform configs

pr:
  branches:
    include:
      - main
  paths:
    include:
      - terraform/**                # <-- CHANGE THIS: match above

variables:
  TF_VERSION: "1.7.0"                              # <-- CHANGE THIS: pin to your version
  TF_WORKING_DIR: "$(System.DefaultWorkingDirectory)/terraform/azure-aks"  # <-- CHANGE THIS
  azureServiceConnection: "my-azure-connection"     # <-- CHANGE THIS: Azure DevOps service connection name

  # Backend config for remote state (Azure Storage)
  backendResourceGroup: "tfstate-rg"                # <-- CHANGE THIS
  backendStorageAccount: "tfstatesa"                # <-- CHANGE THIS
  backendContainer: "tfstate"                       # <-- CHANGE THIS
  backendKey: "terraform.tfstate"                   # <-- CHANGE THIS

pool:
  vmImage: "ubuntu-latest"

stages:
  # =============================================
  # VALIDATE — format check + validation
  # =============================================
  - stage: Validate
    displayName: "Terraform Validate"
    jobs:
      - job: Validate
        displayName: "Format & Validate"
        steps:
          - task: TerraformInstaller@1
            displayName: "Install Terraform $(TF_VERSION)"
            inputs:
              terraformVersion: $(TF_VERSION)

          - script: terraform fmt -check -diff
            displayName: "Terraform Format Check"
            workingDirectory: $(TF_WORKING_DIR)

          - script: terraform init -backend=false
            displayName: "Terraform Init (validation only)"
            workingDirectory: $(TF_WORKING_DIR)

          - script: terraform validate -no-color
            displayName: "Terraform Validate"
            workingDirectory: $(TF_WORKING_DIR)

  # =============================================
  # PLAN — runs on PR and main branch
  # =============================================
  - stage: Plan
    displayName: "Terraform Plan"
    dependsOn: Validate
    jobs:
      - job: Plan
        displayName: "Generate Plan"
        steps:
          - task: TerraformInstaller@1
            displayName: "Install Terraform $(TF_VERSION)"
            inputs:
              terraformVersion: $(TF_VERSION)

          - task: AzureCLI@2
            displayName: "Terraform Init + Plan"
            inputs:
              azureSubscription: $(azureServiceConnection)
              scriptType: bash
              scriptLocation: inlineScript
              workingDirectory: $(TF_WORKING_DIR)
              addSpnToEnvironment: true  # Exposes ARM_CLIENT_ID, ARM_CLIENT_SECRET, etc.
              inlineScript: |
                # Export Azure credentials for Terraform
                export ARM_CLIENT_ID=$servicePrincipalId
                export ARM_CLIENT_SECRET=$servicePrincipalKey
                export ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=$(az account show --query id -o tsv)
                export ARM_TENANT_ID=$tenantId

                # Init with backend
                terraform init -input=false \
                  -backend-config="resource_group_name=$(backendResourceGroup)" \
                  -backend-config="storage_account_name=$(backendStorageAccount)" \
                  -backend-config="container_name=$(backendContainer)" \
                  -backend-config="key=$(backendKey)"

                # Plan
                terraform plan -input=false -no-color -out=tfplan

                # Save human-readable plan for review
                terraform show -no-color tfplan > $(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory)/plan_output.txt

          - publish: $(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory)/plan_output.txt
            artifact: terraform-plan
            displayName: "Publish Plan Output"

  # =============================================
  # APPLY — runs only on main branch, with approval
  # =============================================
  - stage: Apply
    displayName: "Terraform Apply"
    dependsOn: Plan
    condition: and(succeeded(), eq(variables['Build.SourceBranch'], 'refs/heads/main'))
    jobs:
      - deployment: Apply
        displayName: "Apply Infrastructure Changes"
        environment: production     # <-- CHANGE THIS: configure approval gates in Azure DevOps
        strategy:
          runOnce:
            deploy:
              steps:
                - checkout: self

                - task: TerraformInstaller@1
                  displayName: "Install Terraform $(TF_VERSION)"
                  inputs:
                    terraformVersion: $(TF_VERSION)

                - task: AzureCLI@2
                  displayName: "Terraform Init + Apply"
                  inputs:
                    azureSubscription: $(azureServiceConnection)
                    scriptType: bash
                    scriptLocation: inlineScript
                    workingDirectory: $(TF_WORKING_DIR)
                    addSpnToEnvironment: true
                    inlineScript: |
                      export ARM_CLIENT_ID=$servicePrincipalId
                      export ARM_CLIENT_SECRET=$servicePrincipalKey
                      export ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=$(az account show --query id -o tsv)
                      export ARM_TENANT_ID=$tenantId

                      terraform init -input=false \
                        -backend-config="resource_group_name=$(backendResourceGroup)" \
                        -backend-config="storage_account_name=$(backendStorageAccount)" \
                        -backend-config="container_name=$(backendContainer)" \
                        -backend-config="key=$(backendKey)"

                      terraform apply -input=false -auto-approve

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Reusable Docker Build & Push
# WHEN TO USE: Call from any workflow that needs to build and push images
# PREREQUISITES: Container registry accessible from GitHub Actions
# SECRETS NEEDED: registry-token (e.g., GITHUB_TOKEN for GHCR)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Inputs when calling this workflow
# RELATED FILES: ci/github-actions/dotnet/docker-publish.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Reusable Docker Build

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      image-name:
        required: true
        type: string
      dockerfile:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: Dockerfile
      context:
        required: false
        type: string
        default: .
    secrets:
      registry-token:
        required: true

jobs:
  docker-build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.registry-token }}

      - name: Set up Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ghcr.io/${{ inputs.image-name }}
          tags: |
            type=sha
            type=ref,event=branch

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: ${{ inputs.context }}
          file: ${{ inputs.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Reusable Slack Notification
# WHEN TO USE: Call from any workflow to send Slack alerts
# PREREQUISITES: Slack Incoming Webhook URL configured
# SECRETS NEEDED: slack-webhook (Slack Incoming Webhook URL)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Inputs when calling this workflow
# RELATED FILES: notifications/slack-notify.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Reusable Slack Notify

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      status:
        required: true
        type: string  # success | failure | cancelled
      message:
        required: false
        type: string
    secrets:
      slack-webhook:
        required: true

jobs:
  notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Send Slack notification
        uses: slackapi/slack-github-action@v1
        with:
          payload: |
            {
              "text": "${{ inputs.status == 'success' && '✅' || '❌' }} *${{ github.repository }}* — ${{ inputs.message || github.workflow }} (${{ github.ref_name }})",
              "attachments": [{
                "color": "${{ inputs.status == 'success' && 'good' || 'danger' }}",
                "fields": [
                  { "title": "Run", "value": "${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}", "short": false }
                ]
              }]
            }
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.slack-webhook }}
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_TYPE: INCOMING_WEBHOOK

﻿# ============================================================

name: Reusable Security Scan

on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      image-ref:
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  trivy-scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.28.0
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ inputs.image-ref }}
          format: "sarif"
          output: trivy-results.sarif
          severity: "CRITICAL,HIGH"

      - name: Upload Trivy results to GitHub Security
        uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
        with:
          sarif_file: trivy-results.sarif

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Matrix Build Strategy
# WHEN TO USE: Testing across multiple OS / runtime versions
# PREREQUISITES: Node.js project (adapt for other runtimes)
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Matrix values for your target platforms
# RELATED FILES: None
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Matrix Build Example

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build (${{ matrix.os }} / ${{ matrix.node }})
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    strategy:
      fail-fast: false  # Don't cancel other matrix jobs on failure
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        node: ["18", "20"]
        exclude:
          - os: macos-latest
            node: "18"  # Skip this combination

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node ${{ matrix.node }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}

      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Monorepo Affected Services
# WHEN TO USE: Monorepos with multiple services in subdirectories
# PREREQUISITES: dorny/paths-filter action, per-service workflows
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Path filters and service names
# RELATED FILES: None
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Monorepo — Build Affected Services

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  detect-changes:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      user-service: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.user-service }}
      order-service: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.order-service }}
      api-gateway: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.api-gateway }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            user-service:
              - 'services/user-service/**'
            order-service:
              - 'services/order-service/**'
            api-gateway:
              - 'services/api-gateway/**'
              - 'shared/**'  # Shared changes affect gateway

  build-user-service:
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.user-service == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-service.yml
    with:
      service: user-service

  build-order-service:
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.order-service == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-service.yml
    with:
      service: order-service

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Release Please (automated releases)
# WHEN TO USE: Automating changelog, version bumps, and GitHub Releases
# PREREQUISITES: Conventional Commits in your commit messages
# SECRETS NEEDED: NPM_TOKEN (if publishing to npm)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: release-type and publish steps
# RELATED FILES: None
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Release Please

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release-please:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        id: release
        with:
          release-type: node  # or: python, java, go, etc.
          # Reads conventional commits to bump version automatically

      # Only run on release (not on every push)
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        if: ${{ steps.release.outputs.release_created }}

      - name: Publish to npm
        if: ${{ steps.release.outputs.release_created }}
        run: |
          npm ci
          npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — .NET Build & Test
# WHEN TO USE: Any ASP.NET Core / .NET 8+ project
# PREREQUISITES: None (self-contained)
# SECRETS NEEDED: None for CI / CODECOV_TOKEN for coverage upload
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: ci/github-actions/dotnet/sonar-scan.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: .NET Build & Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  DOTNET_VERSION: "8.0.x"

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: ${{ env.DOTNET_VERSION }}

      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release

      - name: Test
        run: dotnet test --no-build --configuration Release \
          --logger trx \
          --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" \
          --results-directory ./TestResults

      - name: Upload test results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: TestResults/

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          directory: TestResults

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — .NET Docker Build & Publish
# WHEN TO USE: Publishing .NET container images to GHCR or ACR
# PREREQUISITES: Dockerfile in the repository
# SECRETS NEEDED: GITHUB_TOKEN (automatic for GHCR)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: .NET Docker Build & Publish

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["v*.*.*"]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  docker:
    name: Build & Push Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=sha,prefix=sha-

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          file: docker/dotnet/Dockerfile.api                 # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — .NET SonarQube/SonarCloud Scan
# WHEN TO USE: .NET projects needing code quality gates and PR decoration
# PREREQUISITES: SonarQube or SonarCloud project configured
# SECRETS NEEDED: SONAR_TOKEN
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: quality/sonar-project.properties
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: .NET SonarQube Scan

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  sonar:
    name: SonarQube Analysis
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # Full history required by SonarQube

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: "8.0.x"

      - name: Install SonarScanner
        run: dotnet tool install --global dotnet-sonarscanner

      - name: Begin SonarQube analysis
        run: |
          dotnet-sonarscanner begin \
            /k:"${{ vars.SONAR_PROJECT_KEY }}" \
            /d:sonar.host.url="${{ vars.SONAR_HOST_URL }}" \
            /d:sonar.token="${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}" \
            /d:sonar.cs.opencover.reportsPaths="**/coverage.opencover.xml"

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build --configuration Release

      - name: Test with coverage
        run: dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage;Format=opencover"

      - name: End SonarQube analysis
        run: dotnet-sonarscanner end /d:sonar.token="${{ secrets.SONAR_TOKEN }}"

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Go Build & Test
# WHEN TO USE: Any Go project with go.mod
# PREREQUISITES: go.mod and go.sum in project root
# SECRETS NEEDED: None / CODECOV_TOKEN for coverage upload
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: docker/go/Dockerfile
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Go Build & Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        go-version: ["1.21", "1.22"]            # <-- CHANGE THIS: your supported Go versions

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Go ${{ matrix.go-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        with:
          go-version: ${{ matrix.go-version }}
          cache: true                            # Caches ~/go/pkg/mod and ~/.cache/go-build

      - name: Download dependencies
        run: go mod download

      - name: Verify dependencies
        run: go mod verify

      - name: Lint with golangci-lint
        uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v4
        with:
          version: latest
          args: --timeout=5m

      - name: Vet
        run: go vet ./...

      - name: Test with coverage
        run: go test -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic ./...

      - name: Check coverage threshold
        run: |
          COVERAGE=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}' | sed 's/%//')
          echo "Total coverage: ${COVERAGE}%"
          THRESHOLD=70                           # <-- CHANGE THIS: your minimum coverage
          if (( $(echo "$COVERAGE < $THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
            echo "::error::Coverage ${COVERAGE}% is below threshold ${THRESHOLD}%"
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Upload coverage
        if: matrix.go-version == '1.22'          # Upload once, not per matrix entry
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          file: coverage.out

      - name: Build
        run: go build -v ./...

  # Build Docker image on main branch
  docker:
    name: Docker Build
    needs: build-test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          file: docker/go/Dockerfile             # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: |
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ github.sha }}
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:latest

# WHEN TO USE: Any Python project with pytest
# PREREQUISITES: requirements.txt and requirements-dev.txt

# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: quality/python/pyproject.toml

# ============================================================

name: Python Build & Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12"]

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}

        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
          cache: "pip"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

      - name: Lint with ruff
        run: ruff check .

      - name: Format check with black
        run: black --check .

      - name: Type check with mypy
        run: mypy .

      - name: Test with pytest
        run: pytest --cov=. --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term-missing

      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          file: coverage.xml

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Python Security Scan
# WHEN TO USE: Python projects needing SAST + dependency vulnerability checks
# PREREQUISITES: requirements.txt
# SECRETS NEEDED: None
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: security/dependency-audit/pip-audit.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Python Security Scan

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 1"  # Every Monday at 06:00 UTC

jobs:
  security:
    name: Security Scan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Install security tools
        run: pip install bandit pip-audit

      - name: Run Bandit (SAST)
        run: bandit -r . -f json -o bandit-report.json --exit-zero  # <-- CHANGE THIS: adjust -r path

      - name: Run pip-audit (dependency CVEs)
        run: pip-audit --format json --output pip-audit-report.json || true

      - name: Upload reports
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: security-reports
          path: |
            bandit-report.json
            pip-audit-report.json

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Ruby on Rails Build & Test
# WHEN TO USE: Any Rails 7+ project
# PREREQUISITES: Gemfile, Gemfile.lock, database.yml with test config
# SECRETS NEEDED: None / CODECOV_TOKEN for coverage upload
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: docker/ruby/Dockerfile.rails
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Rails Build & Test

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  RAILS_ENV: test

jobs:
  build-test:
    name: Build & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    strategy:
      matrix:
        ruby-version: ["3.2", "3.3"]             # <-- CHANGE THIS: your supported Ruby versions

    services:
      postgres:                                   # <-- CHANGE THIS: remove if using SQLite
        image: postgres:16-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: test
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test
          POSTGRES_DB: app_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

      redis:                                      # <-- CHANGE THIS: remove if not using Redis
        image: redis:7-alpine
        ports:
          - 6379:6379
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "redis-cli ping"
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

    env:
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://test:test@localhost:5432/app_test  # <-- CHANGE THIS: match postgres service
      REDIS_URL: redis://localhost:6379/0

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Ruby ${{ matrix.ruby-version }}
        uses: ruby/setup-ruby@v1
        with:
          ruby-version: ${{ matrix.ruby-version }}
          bundler-cache: true                     # Caches gems automatically

      - name: Set up Node.js (for asset pipeline)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"                     # <-- CHANGE THIS: remove if API-only
          cache: yarn                            # <-- CHANGE THIS: use npm if not using Yarn

      - name: Install JS dependencies
        run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile       # <-- CHANGE THIS: remove if API-only

      - name: Set up database
        run: |
          bundle exec rails db:schema:load

      - name: Lint with RuboCop
        run: bundle exec rubocop --parallel

      - name: Security audit with Brakeman
        run: bundle exec brakeman --no-pager --quiet

      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          bundle exec rails test                 # <-- CHANGE THIS: use `bundle exec rspec` for RSpec

      - name: Upload coverage
        if: matrix.ruby-version == '3.3'
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
        with:
          file: coverage/.resultset.json         # <-- CHANGE THIS: path depends on SimpleCov config

  # Build Docker image on main branch
  docker:
    name: Docker Build
    needs: build-test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Build and push
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          file: docker/ruby/Dockerfile.rails     # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Dockerfile
          push: true
          tags: |
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ github.sha }}
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:latest

# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitHub Actions — Terraform Plan / Apply
# WHEN TO USE: Automating Terraform workflows with PR-based plan review
# PREREQUISITES: Cloud credentials configured via OIDC or secrets
# SECRETS NEEDED: Cloud auth (see provider-specific sections below)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Lines marked with # <-- CHANGE THIS
# RELATED FILES: terraform/azure-aks/, terraform/aws-eks/, terraform/gcp-gke/
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

name: Terraform Plan / Apply

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "terraform/**"    # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to your Terraform configs
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "terraform/**"    # <-- CHANGE THIS: match above

# Prevent concurrent Terraform runs against the same state
concurrency:
  group: terraform-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: false   # Never cancel in-progress Terraform applies

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write        # For posting plan output as PR comment
  id-token: write             # For OIDC authentication to cloud providers

env:
  TF_VERSION: "1.7.0"                       # <-- CHANGE THIS: pin to your version
  TF_WORKING_DIR: "terraform/azure-aks"      # <-- CHANGE THIS: path to root module
  TF_VAR_FILE: ""                            # <-- CHANGE THIS: e.g., "environments/prod.tfvars"

  # --- Azure (uncomment if using Azure) ---
  # ARM_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
  # ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}
  # ARM_TENANT_ID: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
  # ARM_USE_OIDC: true

  # --- AWS (uncomment if using AWS) ---
  # AWS_REGION: us-east-1

  # --- GCP (uncomment if using GCP) ---
  # GOOGLE_PROJECT: my-project-id

jobs:
  # =============================================
  # PLAN — runs on every PR
  # =============================================
  plan:
    name: Terraform Plan
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # --- Cloud Authentication (uncomment ONE) ---

      # Azure OIDC
      # - name: Azure Login (OIDC)
      #   uses: azure/login@v2
      #   with:
      #     client-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_CLIENT_ID }}
      #     tenant-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_TENANT_ID }}
      #     subscription-id: ${{ secrets.AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID }}

      # AWS OIDC
      # - name: AWS Login (OIDC)
      #   uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
      #   with:
      #     role-to-assume: ${{ secrets.AWS_DEPLOY_ROLE_ARN }}
      #     aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      # GCP Workload Identity
      # - name: GCP Auth (Workload Identity)
      #   uses: google-github-actions/auth@v2
      #   with:
      #     workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
      #     service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}
          terraform_wrapper: true   # Enables stdout capture for PR comments

      - name: Terraform Format Check
        id: fmt
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: terraform fmt -check -diff
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Terraform Init
        id: init
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: terraform init -input=false

      - name: Terraform Validate
        id: validate
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: terraform validate -no-color

      - name: Terraform Plan
        id: plan
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: |
          PLAN_ARGS="-input=false -no-color -out=tfplan"
          if [ -n "${{ env.TF_VAR_FILE }}" ]; then
            PLAN_ARGS="${PLAN_ARGS} -var-file=${{ env.TF_VAR_FILE }}"
          fi
          terraform plan ${PLAN_ARGS}
        continue-on-error: true

      # Post plan output as a PR comment for review
      - name: Comment Plan on PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const output = `#### 🧹 Terraform Format: \`${{ steps.fmt.outcome }}\`
            #### ⚙️ Terraform Init: \`${{ steps.init.outcome }}\`
            #### ✅ Terraform Validate: \`${{ steps.validate.outcome }}\`

              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: output
            })

        if: steps.plan.outcome == 'failure'
        run: exit 1

  # =============================================
  # APPLY — runs only on push to main (after PR merge)
  # =============================================
  apply:
    name: Terraform Apply
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment: production   # <-- CHANGE THIS: requires environment approval in GitHub settings

      #     aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}

      # - name: GCP Auth (Workload Identity)

      #   with:
      #     workload_identity_provider: ${{ secrets.GCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER }}
      #     service_account: ${{ secrets.GCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT }}

      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TF_VERSION }}

      - name: Terraform Init
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: terraform init -input=false

      - name: Terraform Apply
        working-directory: ${{ env.TF_WORKING_DIR }}
        run: |
          APPLY_ARGS="-input=false -auto-approve"
          if [ -n "${{ env.TF_VAR_FILE }}" ]; then

# WHEN TO USE: Building Docker images in GitLab CI (works in rootless runners)

# Reusable Docker Build job — uses Kaniko (no Docker-in-Docker required)

  stage: package
  image:
    name: gcr.io/kaniko-project/executor:v1.23.2-debug
    entrypoint: [""]
  variables:
    DOCKERFILE: Dockerfile
    IMAGE_TAG: $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA
  script:
    - |
      /kaniko/executor \
        --context "$CI_PROJECT_DIR" \
        --dockerfile "$CI_PROJECT_DIR/$DOCKERFILE" \
        --destination "$IMAGE_TAG" \
        --destination "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest" \
        --cache=true \
        --cache-repo "$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/cache"
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: GitLab CI — Reusable Slack/Teams Notification
# WHEN TO USE: Post-pipeline notifications to messaging platforms
# PREREQUISITES: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL variable configured in GitLab
# SECRETS NEEDED: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Webhook URL and message format
# RELATED FILES: notifications/slack-notify.yml
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================

# Reusable Slack notification job
.notify:
  stage: .post
  image: alpine:latest
  script:
    - apk add --no-cache curl
    - |
      STATUS="${CI_JOB_STATUS:-unknown}"
      COLOR="good"
      if [ "$STATUS" != "success" ]; then COLOR="danger"; fi
      curl -X POST "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d "{\"text\":\"Pipeline $STATUS for $CI_PROJECT_NAME ($CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME)\"}"
  when: always

Templates for each CI platform and technology combination.

## Platform Matrix

| Tech | GitHub Actions | GitLab CI | Azure Pipelines | Jenkins |
|------|---------------|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| .NET | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

## Choosing a Platform

- **GitHub Actions**: Best for repos hosted on GitHub; generous free tier
- **GitLab CI**: Best native option for GitLab repos; powerful include system
- **Azure Pipelines**: Best for teams in the Microsoft ecosystem; tight AKS/ACR integration
- **Jenkins**: When you need full control or have on-premise requirements

## Shared Patterns

- [`github-actions/_shared/`](github-actions/_shared/) — Reusable GitHub Actions workflows
- [`gitlab-ci/_includes/`](gitlab-ci/_includes/) — GitLab CI include templates
- [`azure-pipelines/_templates/`](azure-pipelines/_templates/) — Azure YAML extends templates

﻿# ============================================================
# TEMPLATE: Docker Compose — Annotated Base Template
# WHEN TO USE: Starting point for any new local dev environment
# PREREQUISITES: Docker and Docker Compose v2+
# SECRETS NEEDED: None (uses .env file)
# WHAT TO CHANGE: Adapt services, ports, and volumes to your stack
# RELATED FILES: compose/dotnet-sqlserver/, compose/python-postgres-redis/
# MATURITY: Stable
# ============================================================
#
# NOTE: The top-level 'version:' key is obsolete in Docker Compose v2+
# and has been intentionally omitted. See:
# https://docs.docker.com/compose/compose-file/04-version-and-name/

# Extension fields: define anchors to reuse across services
x-logging: &default-logging
  driver: "json-file"
  options:
    max-size: "10m"
    max-file: "3"

x-restart-policy: &restart-policy
  restart: unless-stopped

services:
  app:
    # Build from local Dockerfile
    build:
      context: .          # Build context (directory sent to Docker daemon)
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        - BUILD_ENV=development  # ARGs available during build only
      target: runtime     # Stop at a specific multi-stage target

    # Or pull a pre-built image:
    # image: myregistry.io/my-app:1.2.3

    container_name: my-app  # Avoid auto-generated names in dev

    ports:
      - "3000:3000"     # host:container — only expose what you need

    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=development
      - DB_HOST=db      # Container names resolve as DNS on the same network

    # Load from .env file (never commit secrets)
    env_file:
      - .env

    volumes:
      # Named volume: managed by Docker, persists between restarts
      - app-data:/app/data
      # Bind mount: reflects local file changes instantly (great for dev)
      - ./src:/app/src:ro   # :ro = read-only inside container

    depends_on:
      db:
        condition: service_healthy  # Wait for healthcheck to pass

    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-qO-", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 20s  # Grace period before first check

    networks:
      - app-net

    logging: *default-logging
    <<: *restart-policy

  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=${DB_USER:-appuser}
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${DB_PASSWORD:?DB_PASSWORD must be set}  # Fails if not set
      - POSTGRES_DB=${DB_NAME:-appdb}
    volumes:
      - db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./db/init.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro  # Run on first start
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${DB_USER:-appuser}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
    networks:
      - app-net
    logging: *default-logging
    <<: *restart-policy

volumes:
  app-data:     # Persists across container restarts
  db-data:

networks:
  app-net:
    driver: bridge
    # For external network (e.g., shared across compose files):
    # external: true
